Under the new pricing structure, the adjustments are as follows: Ticket Type All Days Adult Child/Senior Peak Tram Ticket Return $116 $75 Single $82 $52 Special Combo Ticket Return $182 $118 Single $146 $95 Peak Tram Ruby Special Ticket Return $342 $222 Sky Terrace 428 Ticket $80 $50 Guests may continue to purchase tickets valid until 31 January 2026 at the original fare via the company’s official website or WeChat Mini Programme on or before 31 December 2025. The Peak Tram is dedicated to continuously enhancing the guest experience, maintaining the sustainable development of The Peak Tram, and providing value-driven premium services, further contributing to the tourism industry in Hong Kong. The Peak Tram team expresses gratitude for the support of our guests and we look forward to welcoming everyone to enjoy an unforgettable experience. For more information about the new fare, please visit www.thepeak.com.hk or contact The Peak Tower Guest Services at (852) 2849 0668. ### About The Peak Tram and The Peak Tower The Peak Tram is one of the world's oldest and most famous funicular railways. Opened in May 1888, the double reversible funicular railway, as it is technically known, rises from 33m to 396m above sea level along a 1.27 km track with a gradient of between 4 to 25.7 degrees. As one of Hong Kong's most popular tourist attractions, the Peak Tram terminates at The Peak Tower shopping and entertainment complex, which has some of the world's greatest panoramic views. The Peak Tower is one of Hong Kong’s most recognisable icons and is a leading tourist destination. The Peak Tower also offers exciting signature restaurants with stunning views, as well as a number of innovative casual dining outlets. A unique festival-market-style shopping arcade and spectacular special-effects lighting, both inside and out, also serve to make The Peak Tower one of Hong Kong’s most compelling leisure destinations for residents and tourists alike. Located at 428 metres above sea level, Sky Terrace 428, the highest viewing terrace in Hong Kong offers stunning spectacular panoramic views of our vibrant city – from the stunning cityscape to the tranquillity of green landscapes, across Victoria Harbour and the South China Sea. About The Hongkong and Shanghai Hotels, Limited (Stock Code: 45) Incorporated in 1866 and listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, The Hongkong and Shanghai Hotels, Limited is the holding company of a group which is engaged in the ownership, development, and management of prestigious hotels and commercial and residential properties in key locations in Asia, Europe and the United States, as well as the provision of tourism and leisure, retail and other services. The Peninsula Hotels portfolio comprises The Peninsula Hong Kong, The Peninsula Shanghai, The Peninsula Beijing, The Peninsula Tokyo, The Peninsula London, The Peninsula Paris, The Peninsula Istanbul, The Peninsula New York, The Peninsula Chicago, The Peninsula Beverly Hills, The Peninsula Bangkok and The Peninsula Manila. The property portfolio of the group includes The Repulse Bay Complex, The Peak Tower and St. John’s Building in Hong Kong; The Landmark in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam and 21 avenue Kléber in Paris, France. The Peak Tram, Retail and Others portfolio of the group includes The Peak Tram in Hong Kong; The Quail in Carmel, California; Peninsula Clubs and Consultancy Services, Peninsula Merchandising, and Tai Pan Laundry in Hong Kong. 30. Any tax will be charged on the ticket? Any service charges will be added onto the price if I purchase online?
:   There is no VAT or GST in Hong Kong, therefore no tax on the ticket. We also do not charge any handling fee or service fee for online purchase.

31. If I lost my ticket, can I get a new ticket?
:   We accept no responsibility for any lost ticket. Please read our “Terms of Service” carefully.

1. Which payment method can I choose to buy tickets? / Can I pay by Octopus card? / Can I pre-purchase tickets online?
:   Tickets can be purchased online in advance or at the Peak Tram terminus upon arrival. We accept cash, major credit cards and digital wallets. [...] 23. Can I change the date and timeslot of the tickets I bought online?
:   No, tickets are valid on the date and timeslot specified upon purchase only. The date and timeslot cannot be changed upon order completion unless the purchased service is suspended by the company. Please read our “Terms of Service” carefully before your acceptance.

24. How many days in advance can I buy the tickets online?
:   You can buy tickets online for visit on the same day, up to 30 days in advance.

25. Can I enjoy the discounted rate if I purchase the Peak Tram ticket(s) and Sky Terrace 428 ticket(s) separately?
:   No, you must select the Peak Tram Special Combo tickets to enjoy the best price.

1. Which payment method can I choose to buy tickets? / Can I pay by Octopus card? / Can I pre-purchase tickets online?
:   Tickets can be purchased online in advance or at the Peak Tram terminus upon arrival. We accept cash, major credit cards and digital wallets.

    Wechat Pay is not available for Smartphone online purchase.

    Octopus Cards cannot be used for direct entry. Tickets can be purchased at the on-site ticket counters using an Octopus Card. Passengers boarding at the Intermediate Station may also pay directly on the tram using an Octopus Card.

    At our website, visitors can now enjoy a convenient on-line ticketing experience, click [to start!](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us)

[2. Is there any Car Park at The Peak?](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us)
:   [There is no car parking facility in The Peak Tower. It’s available at an adjacent commercial building at the Peak. You may contact the Peak Galleria at 2849 4113 for more information.](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us)

[3. Are there any luggage storage services at the Peak Tram or The Peak Tower?](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us)
:   [Luggage Storage is not available in the Peak Tram Stations or The Peak Tower.](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us)

[4. Are there any baby care facilities at The Peak Tower?](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us)
:   [The Peak Tower offers baby care facilities in a nursing room. Please contact our staff at Customer Service Counter on ground floor of The Peak Tower for assistance.](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us)

[5. Can a pregnant woman ride the Peak Tram?](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us)
:   [The Peak Tram is committed to providing a safe and pleasant journey for all ages.  Each tramcar provides 167 seats and 43 standing space. If all seats are occupied, it might be a good idea to wait for the next tram.](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us)

[6. Is baby stroller allowed in the Peak Tram?](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us)
:   [Baby strollers must be folded before entering the Peak Tram and the width of baby strollers must not be wider than 26 inches or 66 cm after folded.  Our staff will be happy to provide assistance if needed.](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us)

[7. Are dogs / pets allowed on the Peak Tram and in The Peak Tower?](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us)
:   [Pets are not allowed on the Peak Tram, except qualified guide dogs. While pets in stroller or on a leash are allowed in The Peak Tower.](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us)

[8. Is there a dog garden located at The Peak?](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us)
:   [A dog garden is located at Victoria Peak Garden (opposite to No.40 Mount Austin Road), approximately 1.2 km away from The Peak Tower. Please contact the Leisure and Culture Services Department at 2853 2566 or click](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us) [here](https://www.lcsd.gov.hk/en/facilities/facilitieslist/facilities.php?ftid=47&did=1) for more information.

9. Any free Wi-Fi provided at The Peak Tower?
:   Free Wi-Fi service is available at the Peak Tram termini, The Peak Tower and Sky Terrace 428.  Visitors can instantly share their fabulous photos and happy moments to friends and family through any social media platforms.

10. Can you make any special arrangement for my marriage proposal? / Booking Sky Terrace 428 for Proposal for private function?
:   Marriage proposal service is not available at this moment. You may consider proposing in one of the restaurants in The Peak Tower or renting part of Sky Terrace 428 for your marriage proposal.

    **The Peak Tower's Sky Terrace 428 Rental**

    Sky Terrace 428 West Deck is open for booking as a private venue - an unparalleled choice for private functions, business gatherings and, possibly the city’s "highest" parties!

    **Event & Party Planning**

    The Peak Tower, with its iconic wok-like architecture, offers many great dining options with a view in signature restaurants, leisurely shopping and souvenir hunting as well as thrill entertainment experience - adding more fun and excitement to your itinerary, and making your party an unforgettable memory to your guests.

    Please click [here](http://thepeak.com.hk/en/ticket-and-booking/sky-terrace-428-rental) for more details of Sky Terrace 428 rental.

11. Are there any restrictions on use of photographic tripods at the Peak Tram, The Peak Tower or Sky Terrace 428?
:   For safety reasons, tripods and lighting in the Peak Tram, The Peak Tower and Sky Terrace 428 are not allowed.

12. Is it possible to watch the Victoria harbour fireworks display at Sky Terrace 428?
:   Sky Terrace 428, standing at 428 metres above sea level, is the highest outdoor viewing platform in Hong Kong offering a stunning 360-degree panoramic view across the Victoria Harbour. Whether you visit The Peak in the morning, afternoon or night time, Sky Terrace 428 is definitely a scenic spot should not be missed!

    Crowd control measure will be implemented at Sky Terrace 428 on the day of firework display. For safety reasons, entry may be temporarily suspended without prior notice when necessary.

13. May I fly a drone to film or take photographs?
:   Flying drones on the property of The Peak Tram and The Peak Tower is prohibited without the permission of the management company. Sky Terrace 428 Rental Service is offered which allows you to capture aerial footage of the breathtaking scenery and city landscapes. If you're interested, please email to [[email protected]](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#d3babdb5bc93a7bbb6a3b6b2b8fdb0bcbefdbbb8) for the booking details.

14. What is the arrangement if I encounter sudden tram suspension on the valid day of my pre-purchased ticket?
:   In case of repair, maintenance, inclement weather including but not limit to the hoisting of tropical cyclone signal no.8 or above, emergency or other reasons, The Company may suspend the operation of the Peak Tram or Sky Terrace 428 at any time with or without prior notice to the public. Within 30 days after the service suspension, you can exchange on-day ticket at the Peak Tram terminus ticket office by presenting your unused or partially used ticket.

15. Do I have priority to board the tram with the timed-tickets I purchased online?
:   The timed-tickets are designed to help manage the flow of visitors, but they do not provide priority boarding. Timeslot tickets are only available through our official online ticketing platform. Please arrive at the Peak Tram Central Terminus within the timeslot stated on your tickets. The queueing time will depend on the number of visitors on the day.

16. What languages are supported in the Online Ticketing platform?
:   The platform supports English, Traditional Chinese and Simplified Chinese.

17. What are web browser cookie and how are they used in the Online Ticketing platform?
:   Please click [here](https://www.thepeak.com.hk/en/data-privacy-security-policy) for the Data Privacy Policies and Cookie Policies details.

18. Why I do not receive my e-Tickets after booking confirmed?
:   The e-ticket is available for download on the “Order Information” page after payment success and will be sent to your provided email address upon completion of payment. If you have not received the email in 5 minutes, please check your Spam or Junk mail folder.

19. During online purchase process, I have progressed to the payment gateway but the system does not respond.  How can I find out if the transaction is successful or not?
:   The transaction is complete only when it proceeds to “Order Information” page with an “Download e-ticket” available, or when you receive an e-ticket in your email. Your card will not be charged if the transaction doesn’t complete.

20. Is my online payment secure?
:   We’re using a third party secure payment gateway to process your payment. The payment gateway is fully compliant with Payment Card Industry Data Security Standards (PCI DSS).

21. How do I use the e-ticket at the gate?
:   You can present the QR code on your e-ticket from your Smartphone or in paper format at the gate. Please set your phone’s screen brightness to the highest before scanning the QR code. Best scanning distance is around 2-3cm from the gate sensor.

22. Can I get a refund if I cancel my trip?
:   No, all tickets sold are non-refundable. Please refer to “[Terms of Service](/node/614)” for details.

23. Can I change the date and timeslot of the tickets I bought online?
:   No, tickets are valid on the date and timeslot specified upon purchase only. The date and timeslot cannot be changed upon order completion unless the purchased service is suspended by the company. Please read our “[Terms of Service](/node/614)” carefully before your acceptance.

24. How many days in advance can I buy the tickets online?
:   You can buy tickets online for visit on the same day, up to 30 days in advance.

25. Can I enjoy the discounted rate if I purchase the Peak Tram ticket(s) and Sky Terrace 428 ticket(s) separately?
:   No, you must select the Peak Tram Special Combo tickets to enjoy the best price.

26. Can I purchase concession ticket other than child and senior tickets through online booking?
:   No, Concession rate applies to holders of "Registration Card for people with Disabilities" issued by the Central Registry for Rehabilitation (CRR), Labour and Welfare Bureau of HKSAR; and only for ticket purchase on arrival. (Except 3/F I-Dragon World ticket counter)

    Proof of the registration card may be required upon ticket purchase.

27. How can I reserve a half/whole tram?
:   Please email to [[email protected]](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection) for enquiry. Confirmation is subject to receipt of full payment at least 5 working days prior to your visit. Please allow sufficient time for processing.

28. Can I order e-tickets with my credit card and give them to friends/guests as a gift?
:   Yes, we do not collect ticket users’ personal information during online purchase process. The e-ticket holders simply scan the QR code at our gate for entry.

29. Any discount if I purchase 10+ tickets at a time? How many tickets can I purchase?
:   Discounts are offered to companies or groups’ booking for 20 or more visitors, please email to [[email protected]](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection) for enquiry.

    For individual booking or group booking less than 20 people, the standard published rate applies, please click [here](https://webstore.thepeak.com.hk/?lang=en_us) for details.

    You can purchase up to a maximum of 10 tickets per ticket type per adult, child, senior at each transaction. If you need to purchase more, you will need to do a separate online transaction.

30. Any tax will be charged on the ticket? Any service charges will be added onto the price if I purchase online?
:   There is no VAT or GST in Hong Kong, therefore no tax on the ticket. We also do not charge any handling fee or service fee for online purchase.

31. If I lost my ticket, can I get a new ticket?
:   We accept no responsibility for any lost ticket. Please read our “[Terms of Service](/node/614)” carefully.

    E-tickets sent to purchaser’s email shall be kept securely until tickets are used or expired. No re-issuance can be arranged.

32. Will the Peak Tram and Sky Terrace close during inclement weather?
:   Yes. If a Typhoone no. 8 or higher or special inclement weather warning is hoisted, the Peak Tram and Sky Terrace will close. [Full details are here](https://www.thepeak.com.hk/en/node/557).

33. How do I know which day is regular or peak day?
:   You can find the up-to-date calendar of regular and peak days [**here**](https://www.thepeak.com.hk/sites/peak/files/2023-12/20231212%20Detailed%20PDF_v2.pdf).

34. Will there be any crowd control management implemented during peak hours?
:   During public holidays or festive seasons, crowd control management will be implemented. Visitors are encouraged to purchase tickets in advance and come before 3pm during non-peak hours.

35. How do I know the quality of the view at the Peak?
:   The quality of the view cannot be guaranteed under certain circumstances such as adverse weather. If you wish to check the weather condition before visiting the Peak, please visit the real-time weather photos captured by The Hong Kong Observatory:

    * Views looking towards the east: [https://www.hko.gov.hk/en/wxinfo/ts/webcam/VPA\_photo.htm](https://www.hko.gov.hk/en/wxinfo/ts/webcam/VPA_photo.htm "https://www.hko.gov.hk/en/wxinfo/ts/webcam/VPA_photo.htm")

    * Views looking towards the north-northeast: [https://www.hko.gov.hk/en/wxinfo/ts/webcam/VPB\_photo.htm](https://www.hko.gov.hk/en/wxinfo/ts/webcam/VPB_photo.htm "https://www.hko.gov.hk/en/wxinfo/ts/webcam/VPB_photo.htm")

36. How do wheelchair users get to the Peak Tram Central Terminus?
:   To provide a more enjoyable, efficient and comfortable experience, Peak Tramways has completed a significant upgrade project, while also providing a more accessible experience for wheelchair users. For more information, please visit [the People of Fortitude website](http://www.pof.org.hk/accessibility-info-place-detail.php?page=1&id=150).

## Frequently Asked Questions

How do you pronounce _the_?

There are two main ways to pronounce this little word, based largely on what word follows it. If _the_ is followed by a word which begins with a consonant sound (such as _water_), then it's pronounced \t͟hə\—that is, with the same vowel sound as the word _of_. However, if the word following it begins with a vowel sound (such as _orange_), _the_ is usually pronounced \t͟hē\ (to rhyme with _me_) (but is also sometimes pronounced \t͟hə\, as above).

Is _the_ the most common word in English?

Probably. Vocabulary varies from person to person, and there are likewise differences between the words used in spoken and written language, but _the_ tends to top the list of most commonly used words found in collections of written text.

Can _the_ be trademarked?

It can be attempted, as The Ohio State University tried to do in 2019; the school was unsuccessful in this attempt. The Ohio State was not the first entity to seek a trademark for the word _the_; clothing designer Marc Jacobs has also asked the patent office for a trademark on this word.

## Word History

Etymology

Definite article and Preposition

Middle English, from Old English _thē_, masculine demonstrative pronoun & definite article, alteration (influenced by oblique cases — as _thæs_, genitive — & neuter, _thæt_) of _sē_; akin to Greek _ho_, masculine demonstrative pronoun & definite article — more at [that](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/that)

Adverb

Middle English, from Old English _thȳ_ by that, instrumental of _thæt_ that

Combining form

Middle English _theo-_, from Latin, from Greek _the-, theo-_, from _theos_

First Known Use

Definite Article

before the 12th century, in the meaning defined at [sense 1a](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/the#h1)

Adverb

before the 12th century, in the meaning defined at [sense 1](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/the#h2)

Preposition

15th century, in the meaning defined [above](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/the#h3)

Time Traveler

Do you pronounce the word 'the' correctly?  
  
Before a consonant (p,b,m,s.. etc), the word THE is pronounced as th' with a schwa sound :)  
  
Before a vowel, the word THE is often pronounced like 'thee'.
